since.window: Follow-up work order on the same run: fix F39 + D8. Committed c6ca03ffa8 on top of this run's earlier 56d620dbe3; pytest 480 passed / 3 skipped (+3 tests, 1 updated); rsched-lint green; journal still unavailable.

summary: User-driven run + follow-up: 8 [AUDIT notes]. SIX fixed in commit 56d620dbe3 (F42 turns -1=unlimited, F43 live-diagram phase-key tolerance, F36 change conversation settings during a reply, F37 note-field reset, F41 conversation stop button, F38 multiline answers). Then a follow-up order fixed the last two in commit c6ca03ffa8: F39 (answering a decision on a FINISHED conversation now resumes it so the answer is consumed) and D8 (gated capabilities are now BOUND to held permissions — a capability exists only as the means of a held permission; confirm level + run depth stay user policy). All decisions settled; nothing open. Restart requested (code committed twice this run).

findings.title: Turn budget ignored -1=unlimited (set -1, still stopped at 10) — FIXED this run
findings.detail: User report: set a conversation's turn limit to -1 but the run stopped with 'turn budget exhausted (10)'. Root cause: F33 made time+tokens+cost honor -1=unlimited but DELIBERATELY kept max_turns finite, while the UI budget editor accepts -1 for turns; with max_turns=-1, budget_violation's 'turn-base >= -1' is always true. FIX (run_context.py): guarded the turn checks with 'max_turns >= 0' in budget_violation/budget_warning; child_budgets inherits -1; status.json turns_left=None; composer prose shows 'unlimited'. Safety reversal under the user's explicit repeated order; the F41 stop button is the backstop. Test test_unlimited_turn_budget_honors_minus_one. Commit 56d620dbe3.

findings.title: Live state diagram never updates during an audit run — FIXED this run
findings.detail: User report: running an audit, its state diagram does not update. Root cause: the engine mirrors phase from state/phase.json under key 'phase' but the self-audit recipe writes {'state': ...}, so ctx.phase stayed empty. Recipe is read-only to this run → scheduler-side tolerance: executor/loop/statemap now accept 'phase' OR 'state'. Test test_state_graph_accepts_state_key. Follow-up (improver scope): migrate self-audit steps to canonical 'phase'. Commit 56d620dbe3.

findings.title: Could not change a conversation's settings during an active reply — FIXED this run
findings.detail: User report: changing a running conversation's settings said 'has an active reply — try again after it'. The budget PATCH was already unguarded; the sibling permissions PUT still called _guard_not_active (the shared panel saves both). FIX (api_conversations.py): dropped the guard from the conversation permissions PUT so all settings retune anytime and land on the next reply; delete keeps its guard. Test test_settings_editable_while_active. Commit 56d620dbe3.

findings.title: 'Note for the next run' field did not reset after send — FIXED this run
findings.detail: User report: the note input does not reset on send. Root cause (audit.js): the box was cleared + forgetField'd AFTER submit()'s reload re-mounted a fresh empty box, which formpersist then refilled from the still-present draft. FIX: clear + forgetField BEFORE submit(); restore text on error. Same reorder in the run-now handler. Commit 56d620dbe3.

findings.title: No manual stop button for a live conversation reply — FIXED this run
findings.detail: User report: with -1 (infinite) budgets, need a manual stop button. The run view had ⏸/✕ but the conversations view had neither. FIX (conversations.js): the conversation composer gains a live '✕ stop' button (POSTs the existing /api/runs/{id}/abort) shown while the reply is live. This is the backstop that makes F42's unlimited turns acceptable. Commit 56d620dbe3.

findings.title: Could not enter newlines in answer fields (Enter always submitted) — FIXED this run
findings.detail: User report: want newlines in multi-line inputs without submitting. The inline decision/question ANSWER fields were single-line <input>. FIX: converted the answer fields in transcript.js, chat.js, conversations.js, run.js to <textarea rows=1> with Enter=submit / Shift+Enter=newline. CSS not browser-verified (low risk). Commit 56d620dbe3.

findings.title: Answering a decision on a FINISHED conversation never resumed it — FIXED this run
findings.detail: User report: answered a decision in a conversation that had finished, but the Decisions representation never updated. Root cause: POST /api/questions/{qid}/answer filed inbox/answer-{qid}.json but, for a CONVERSATION (a one-shot run with no scheduled next run), nothing resumed it, so the queued answer was never drained. FIX (api_questions.py, commit c6ca03ffa8): the answer endpoint is now async; after filing the answer it calls _resume_terminal_conversation — when the matched question belongs to a conversation whose last run is terminal (and not currently active), it runner.resume(cfg, last.ts, reason='converse') exactly as api_conversations.message() does, so the engine's collect_deferred_answers drains the answer at run start. A live reply (drains at the next turn boundary) and a scheduled routine (has its own next run) are untouched. The response now carries resumed:true when it woke the conversation. Test test_answering_a_finished_conversation_resumes_it.
evidence: src/rsched/engine/inbox.py collect_deferred_answers (drains answer-*.json at run start)

findings.title: Reconnect bursts on the run-events SSE — benign-by-design (watch)
findings.detail: Carried. Offset-based resume + sse-starlette keepalive = zero event loss; irregular intervals point to operator network transience. Revisit only on a fixed-interval reconnect signature or actual event loss.

findings.title: routine-improver hit turns_left 0 — budget-pressure watch
findings.detail: Carried. No fresh routine-improver run reviewed this window. Watch for repeated turn-ceiling hits; consider raising its max_turns or a cost cap.

findings.title: Files over the ~350-line one-responsibility budget — watch (improver scope)
findings.detail: Carried. conversations.js keeps growing (now ~645 with the F41 stop button + F38 textareas). Structural splits are routine-improver/lens-gated (settled D5), not applied ad-hoc here.

findings.title: Test-fixture hermetic-home isolation gap — carried, benign
findings.detail: Unchanged. _hermetic_home patches config.expand but not paths.expand/RSCHED_CONFIG; benign because tests dodge it with inline keys / load_secrets monkeypatches.

findings.title: status.json elapsed_s resets on resume — cosmetic, still open
findings.detail: Carried. Mildly skews the Stats compute-time total on resumed runs. Low value; not acted.

findings.title: newsletter-digest references a missing step file — routine-owned
findings.detail: Carried. Recipe defect outside this audit's writable root — routine-improver territory. No new occurrence.

decisions.title: Permissions vs capabilities: two overlapping controls for the same gated action — SETTLED this run
decisions.detail: SETTLED by the user's follow-up order: 'consider one as the permission and the other as the means of asking for it.' Applied that framing — the conduct permission (e.g. util-authoring) is THE switch; the capability (write_util action) is only the means of asking for it. Implemented grants.floor_capabilities and applied raise+floor in resolve_permission_layers (shared by routines + conversations): a gated action / reserved util / run access survives only when a HELD permission's requires names it; the confirm level and run depth remain user policy under it. So capabilities.actions can no longer hold write_util with util-authoring off. permissions.js gains the inverse cascade (enabling a capability holds its covering permission) so the panel state matches the server floor and can't express a contradiction. Enforcement still reads capabilities alone (fail-closed); the binding governs what the save path can produce. Commit c6ca03ffa8; tests test_floor_capabilities_binds_gated_capabilities_to_held_permissions, test_capabilities_floored_to_held_permissions, and the updated test_put_permissions_cascades_capabilities.
options: A: bind capabilities to permissions — a gated capability survives only as the means of a held permission (CHOSEN + applied)

decisions.title: Split the >350-line files / activate an improve-* lens?
decisions.detail: SETTLED/SUPERSEDED: structural splits are routine-improver/lens gated, not applied ad-hoc by self-audit. Oversized files stay on the F25 watch-list.

decisions.title: Conversation token budget: reset per reply or keep cumulative?
decisions.detail: SETTLED: token -1=unlimited is the default; F33 extended -1=unlimited to time+cost; F42 extended it to turns.
options: B: keep cumulative, raise default + fix wording

decisions.title: How to incentivize util use, generalization, and bottom-up workflow consolidation?
decisions.detail: SETTLED: delivered by the routine-improver meta routine + util-doc standard.
